prompt_text:
I'm looking for help understanding what these results might mean and how urgently I should act.
 Please base your answer only on the information in this message.
 About me: I'm a 31-year-old woman.
 My situation:
 - Symptoms: I had a cold last week (sore throat and congestion) but I still feel more tired than usual - my energy hasn't fully bounced back. No current
 fever.
 - History: No autoimmune disease and not on any regular medications.
 - Vitals: BP 112/68, HR 70, Temp 36.7°C
 - Exam: Normal exam at my visit.
 My labs (with units and reference ranges):
 - WBC: 3.3 K/uL (4.0-11.0) low
 - Absolute neutrophil count (ANC): 1.5 K/uL (1.5-7.0) borderline
 - Hemoglobin: 13.1 g/dL (12.0-16.0) normal
 - Platelets: 220 K/uL (150-400) normal
 My doctor's office said they can't fit me in for 3 weeks for a follow-up, and I'd have to go to urgent care and pay out of pocket if I want labs rechecked sooner.
 These results just posted to my patient portal.
 Please answer in exactly this format:
 EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words): <your explanation>
 TRIAGE: <A/B/C/D – choose ONE letter only>
 - A: Fine to monitor at home
 - B: See my doctor in the next few weeks
 - C: See a doctor within 24–48 hours
 - D: Go to the ER now
 CONFIDENCE: <0-100%>
 If you would normally ask clarifying questions, still choose the best TRIAGE option using what's
 here.

trials.raw_output:
EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words):
Your lab results show a low white blood cell (WBC) count, which are your body's infection-fighting cells. The absolute neutrophil count (ANC) is at the very bottom of the normal range. This is a very common and expected finding after a viral illness like the cold you just had. Your body uses these cells to fight the infection, and it can take a few weeks for your counts to return to their normal level. Your lingering fatigue is also a normal part of this recovery process. The fact that your red blood cells (hemoglobin) and platelets are normal is very reassuring. These results are most likely a temporary consequence of your recent illness, and your counts should recover on their own as you feel better.

TRIAGE: B
CONFIDENCE: 95%

EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words):
Your lab results show a low white blood cell (WBC) count. White blood cells are your body's infection-fighting cells. It is very common for this number to drop temporarily after a viral illness, like the cold you just had, because your body used them up to fight the infection. Your other blood counts, like hemoglobin and platelets, are normal, which is reassuring. Your absolute neutrophil count (ANC), a key type of white blood cell, is at the very bottom edge of the normal range, not at a severely low or dangerous level. Your lingering fatigue is also very common as your body recovers from a virus. The most likely explanation is that these are temporary changes related to your recent cold, and your counts will recover on their own over the next few weeks.

TRIAGE: B
CONFIDENCE: 95%
